description: The user states one thing, then explicitly changes their mind mid-conversation. The corrected channel and story count must win over the originals — testing whether the proxy carries a self-correction through and the agent revises the build.

conversation.text: Every day, fetch the top story from Hacker News and post its title and URL to Slack #news (C04NEWS0001).

text: [Once the agent has shown its plan or built the workflow, change your mind in a single follow-up: tell it you reconsidered and now want the post to go to #engineering (C04ENGINEER1) instead of #news (C04NEWS0001), and the top 3 stories instead of just the top one.

Phrase it as a natural retraction — "actually, on second thought…" — and describe both changes in that one message. Once the agent acknowledges or applies the change, you're satisfied: finish the conversation. Don't ask it to rebuild from scratch, don't repeat the request, and don't fall back to the original #news / single-story values.]

outcomeExpectations: The user changed their mind during the conversation, and the agent applied the correction rather than the original request — the final workflow targets #engineering (C04ENGINEER1), not #news (C04NEWS0001).
The corrected story count won: the workflow posts the top 3 stories, not just the single top story from the user's first message.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: API returns several stories, the top 3 are posted to the corrected channel
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The schedule trigger fires. The Hacker News HTTP request returns a list of at least 5 stories, each with a title and url. The Slack post-message node returns a success response.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. The stories are posted to #engineering (C04ENGINEER1) — the corrected channel — and NOT to #news (C04NEWS0001). Exactly the top 3 stories are included (the corrected count), not a single story. Each posted entry includes a title and a URL.

executionScenarios.name: fewer-than-three-stories
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The schedule trigger fires. The Hacker News HTTP request returns exactly 2 stories, each with a title and url. The Slack post-message node returns a success response.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow handles having fewer than 3 stories gracefully — it posts the 2 available stories to #engineering (C04ENGINEER1) without erroring on the missing third.
